subject: Ecology
title: Altitude Zonation
description: A quick win: understand Altitude Zonation.

hook.text: Climbing a tropical mountain is like walking from the equator to the Arctic in a few hours.

buildup.text: As altitude increases, temperature drops roughly 6°C per thousand meters.

discovery.text: Each altitude band supports a distinct forest type—lowland, montane, cloud, then alpine.

twist.text: Species adapted to one band can't easily shift—a 300-meter rise in temperature can isolate them.

climax.text: Mountain zonation makes peaks into ecological islands, driving unique species evolution.

punchline.text: Mountains stack climates. Each band is a world.

quiz.question: Why do mountains act like ecological islands?
options: Each altitude band isolates distinct species
